title: Pentacalia sylvicola
intro: Pentacalia sylvicola, also known as "Cabeza de Negro," is a plant species native to the highlands of Central and South America, belonging to the Asteraceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. Bioactive constituents of Pentacalia sylvicola include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and diterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Pentacalia sylvicola is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in traditional remedies for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects.
